conflictOfInterest.details: The founding-stage accountable principal github:VSBDev also operates the disclosed author agent and this fresh isolated reviewer agent. The adopted quorum policy permits that arrangement; this run did not use the authoring conversation, another review conclusion, or an adjudication draft.

evidenceChecked: Frozen identity: repository HEAD, lesson metadata, and every candidate pack blob at commit 82ef7e209fc3d16273ac7bb1279905bdcde52fcc; no post-candidate lesson-content change was present on PR 5.

All 11 declared content/*.md scenes in lesson.json order: source headings, lists, tables, prompt-before-feedback sequence, accessibility alternatives, recovery route, privacy boundary, and source notes.

assessment.json: four untimed retakeable item paths, labelled single-/multiple-choice and text responses, equivalent-case retry, feedback, rubrics, privacy constraint, and keyboard/text transfer requirement.

lesson.json, ATTRIBUTION.md, AUTHORING-BRIEF.md, references.json, claims.json, and glossary.json: author disclosure, CC BY 4.0 grant, source scope, rights basis, agent access, terminology, and thirdPartyAssets: [].

Pinned source trace at commit 76882799c7614eeb9307687bceab33896a79bb13: syllabus sections 3.2, 4, 7, 18; content-standard sections 2, 5.1, 6-8, 13; evidence synthesis sections 4, 6-9, 12; graph nodes/links.

Original-expression comparison against the four declared likely sources: lesson prose, tables, fictional records, examples, questions, routes, and answer logic use an independent instructional structure; no quotation, adapted asset, or protected dataset extraction found.

Security/privacy scan: only regular 100644 text/JSON files; no assets, diagrams, SVG, TeX, code fences, raw HTML, scripts, event handlers, unsafe schemes, remote embeds, path traversal, secrets, patient data, or identifiable learner records.

Targeted deterministic check: node scripts/validate-lessons.mjs reported one valid production pack plus the excluded specimen; exact source-note, schema, path, assessment, rights, and provenance contracts passed.

Time-boxed Chromium reader check with unchanged candidate content: 1280x720 semantic/print/reduced-motion path, 390x844 narrow keyboard assessment path, and 640 CSS-pixel reflow proxy for 200% zoom from a 1280 baseline.

Rendered checks: 11 scenes loaded in declared order, four semantic tables with 15 header cells, no skipped heading levels or unlabeled assessment controls, no document-level horizontal overflow, print exposed all frames, and answer feedback worked by keyboard.

findings.finding: The complete classify–justify–route operation is available through source-order prose, semantic tables, ordered-sentence table guidance, named-option or structured-text responses, untimed attempts, equivalent retries, and format substitution without lowering the reasoning standard. No outcome depends on color, position, motion, sound, hover, drag, handwriting, speed, or disclosure of private data.

In the available narrow reader shell, opening the Contents drawer by keyboard made the drawer visible but left focus on the trigger instead of moving it to the drawer's close control or current-scene link. Sequential scene controls and direct assessment controls remained keyboard operable, so this did not block equivalent access to the lesson, but the focus transition was not sensible.
findings.resolution: Before production reader exposure, move focus into the opened drawer and return it to the trigger on close, then retest at narrow widths. This is a shared renderer correction and does not require rewriting lesson content.

findings.finding: Each scene source has one logical level-one heading and no skipped source levels. In the available reader integration, however, the level-one heading from ATTRIBUTION.md is embedded beneath the synthesis and references scenes, producing a second level-one heading in each rendered scene and weakening screen-reader heading navigation.
findings.resolution: Render attribution as a separately labelled document/landmark or demote its embedded heading relative to the containing scene. This can be fixed in the shared renderer without changing the reviewed lesson source.

findings.finding: All four sources are pinned repository-owned materials displayed as CC BY 4.0 and recorded for facts-only use with dated checked-no-agent-restriction-found observations. Exact source paths and locators resolve at the pinned commit. No human-only source, third-party asset, quotation, media reuse, dataset extraction, close paraphrase, or contradictory attribution was found; accountable author and material-agent provenance are disclosed consistently.

The candidate uses fictional, node-level records and explicitly excludes names, grades, disability details, health information, diagnosis, admissions, credentials, and private educational records. It supplies no executable or active content and keeps external source links confined to the structured rights registry and attribution record.

limitations: This is an accessibility-and-project-rights policy review, not legal advice. Copyright, database, privacy, consent, and licence conclusions can vary by jurisdiction; qualified counsel remains necessary where the project policy identifies uncertainty.

No representative screen reader, voice-control suite, disabled-learner study, or human usability session was available. Manual semantic review, deterministic checks, and headless Chromium are evidence, not proof of complete accessibility.

There is no direct production lesson reader route. The built production detail payload is tagged schemaVersion 2 while the available specimen reader accepts tag 3, so the interface check normalized only that outer compiled tag in memory; candidate prose, structure, assessment, and metadata were unchanged.
Non-web PDF, EPUB, DOCX, and JATS exporters are not implemented, so export parity was assessed from semantic source and print behavior rather than end-to-end artifacts.

The shared reader's narrow-drawer focus and embedded-attribution heading issues were observed in the time box and recorded as minor findings; no corrected production interface was available for retest.
Originality was compared with the four declared and most likely sources, not an exhaustive internet-wide plagiarism search. No protected third-party expression or asset was declared or found.

The instructions digest is SHA-256 over the exact discloseable user review task text with no terminal newline; hidden provider and system instructions are excluded because they cannot be exported. Agent identity and run provenance are disclosed and attested, not independently verified.
